title: PATCH /api/user/profile — update name and email
description: Implement the endpoint to update the user's display name and/or email address.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/user/profile updates name and/or email and returns updated UserResponse
Fields are optional — only provided fields are updated
Changing to an email already used by another account returns 400
Updated values are reflected in subsequent GET /api/auth/me calls
Returns 401 if no valid JWT provided

steps.details: Create src/api/schemas/user.py with UpdateProfileRequest(BaseModel): name (str | None = None, min_length=1), email (EmailStr | None = None). Create src/api/routers/user.py with APIRouter(prefix='/api/user', tags=['user']).

In user router, add PATCH /profile route. Handler: if request.name is not None, set current_user.name = request.name. If request.email is not None, check no other user has that email (raise 400 if conflict), set current_user.email = request.email. Commit and refresh. Return UserResponse.

Add user router to main.py. Test: PATCH /api/user/profile with {"name": "New Name"} — verify name updated. PATCH with {"email": "new@example.com"} — verify email updated. PATCH with email of another existing user — verify 400. GET /api/auth/me — verify changes persisted.

technicalNotes: Use Optional fields (None default) so clients can update only one field at a time
Check email uniqueness before updating to avoid constraint violations
